description: JitFrames TraceThisAndArguments — Truncates Trace Range to nactuals, Missing WasmValueBox Tracing for Rectifier-Padded Formal externref Slots. CVE-2024-3857 (sec-high). js::jit::TraceThisAndArguments in js/src/jit/JitFrames.cpp is the JSJitToWasm frame tracer used during minor/compacting GC to mark the arguments live for the duration of a JS→wasm call. It iterates over `[0, nactuals)` — the number of *actual* arguments passed by the caller — rather than `[0, nformals)` — the number of declared wasm formal parameters. When a JS caller invokes a wasm export with fewer arguments than the wasm function declares (e.g. calling a `(param externref externref externref)` export with a single argument), the wasm JIT entry stub's arguments rectifier pushes `undefined` JS values for each missing slot and the wasm-side thunk boxes each of those `undefined`s into a freshly nursery-allocated `WasmValueBox` written into the formal argument slot. The trailing `WasmValueBox` objects live in the JIT frame starting at offset `nactuals` and past, and the JSJitToWasm frame keeps them live via those slot writes — but `TraceThisAndArguments` stops its trace loop at `nactuals`, so those boxes are never reported to the tracer. A minor GC sweeps the unmarked nursery `WasmValueBox`es while the wasm frame is still on the call stack holding their pointers; when the wasm function subsequently reads one of its formal parameters (or when the post-call cleanup unboxes them) the dereference lands in freed nursery storage — use-after-free, ASAN reports `heap-use-after-free` on `WasmValueBox::value`. Reachable from any JS caller that can make `Function.prototype.apply`/`.call` pass mismatched arities to a wasm export with externref/anyref/eqref formals. The fix (changeset 7c9c4691c2a3) simplifies TraceThisAndArguments to iterate over all `nformals` slots and trace each one unconditionally, eliminating the truncated-trace window.
